title: Estonia Citizen's Passport Application
description: The application an Estonian citizen files with the Police and Border Guard Board (Politsei- ja Piirivalveamet, PPA) for an Estonian citizen's passport (a biometric travel document valid for crossing the state border), whether applying for the first time, renewing an expiring passport, replacing a lost/stolen/destroyed/unusable one, or updating one after a change of personal data. Sourced directly from the PPA's own bilingual 'APPLICATION FOR IDENTITY DOCUMENTS' AcroForm PDF — a single combined application the PPA publishes for five document types (identity card, digital identity card, travel document, additional passport, seafarer's discharge book); this document is scoped to the travel-document ('Pass') track only, since that is the track an Estonian citizen's passport is requested through. Submitted in person at a PPA service office (this document's scope); the same data can also be filed online through the self-service portal for a renewal, or with a consular agent of Estonia while abroad — both out of scope for this v1.0.0 (see VERIFICATION.md). GovSchema is independent and is not affiliated with, endorsed by, or operated by the Government of Estonia or the Police and Border Guard Board.

authority.name: Politsei- ja Piirivalveamet (Police and Border Guard Board)

source.documentRef: APPLICATION FOR IDENTITY DOCUMENTS (eng-dok-taotlus.pdf) — PPA's official bilingual (Estonian/English) AcroForm application, retrieved directly (HTTP 200, no login, real AcroForm widgets, not a scanned image). Field-by-field guidance cross-referenced against the PPA's own 'Instructions for completing application form' page (https://www.politsei.ee/en/instructions-for-completing-application-form) and its 'Estonian passport for an adult' service pages (https://www.politsei.ee/en/instructions/estonian-passport-for-an-adult, its 'Applying for a passport at a service office' and 'Applying at the self-service portal' sub-pages), all retrieved directly (HTTP 200, no login, server-rendered plain English HTML). Governing law cross-referenced against the Identity Documents Act (Isikut tõendavate dokumentide seadus), official English translation, Riigi Teataja, version in force from 01.10.2025 (RT I, in force until 14.11.2025) — Chapter 3 (§§9-15², issue/verification procedure and the data a document may carry) and Chapter 6 (§§21-21¹, 24-25, Estonian citizen's travel documents). riigiteataja.ee itself is a fully client-rendered Angular SPA that returns an identical empty shell to an unauthenticated fetch, including its own '/tolge/pdf/' official-translation-PDF route; the Act's text was instead retrieved from the Wayback Machine's snapshot of that route (2 October 2025), this registry's established workaround for the same host (see ee/rik/private-limited-company-foundation, GOV-1705).

verification.notes: GOV-1712: GovSchema Standard Research cycle. Opens Estonia's Passport vertical (3rd EE schema, alongside ee/ppa/e-residency-application, GOV-1698, and ee/rik/private-limited-company-foundation, GOV-1705), picked as this cycle's top-priority candidate per the issue's own screening order since PPA — the same agency behind the already-published e-Residency schema — publishes a real, unauthenticated, directly downloadable AcroForm application (a stronger source shape than either existing EE schema, neither of which had a downloadable form and instead relied on the governing-statute-as-primary-source technique alone). See VERIFICATION.md for the full source inventory, field-by-field citations to both the form's own AcroForm field names and the Identity Documents Act's section numbers, and every disclosed judgment call.

fields.name: givenName
fields.label: Given name or names
fields.description: To be completed in capital letters, in Latin letters in the same form as in the applicant's identity document.
fields.sourceRef: Application form, Personal Data section, field 'Eesnimi või -nimed1'

fields.name: surname
fields.label: Surname or names
fields.description: To be completed in capital letters, in Latin letters in the same form as in the applicant's identity document.
fields.sourceRef: Application form, Personal Data section, field 'Perekonnanimi või -nimed1'

fields.name: personalIdentificationCodeOrDateOfBirth
fields.label: Estonian personal code or date of birth (dd/mm/yyyy)
fields.description: Estonian personal identification code (isikukood), or, in its absence, the applicant's date of birth.
fields.sourceRef: Application form, Personal Data section, field 'Isikukood või sünniaeg1'; Identity Documents Act §9(3)(2)-(3)

fields.name: gender
fields.label: Gender
fields.description: Applicant's gender, one of two checkbox options on the form.
fields.sourceRef: Application form, Personal Data section, field 'sugu'; Identity Documents Act §9(3)(5)

fields.name: countryOfBirth
fields.label: Country of birth
fields.description: The current name of the applicant's country of birth.
fields.sourceRef: Application form, Personal Data section, field 'Sünniriik'; Identity Documents Act §9(3)(2)

fields.name: citizenship
fields.label: Citizenship or citizenships
fields.description: The applicant's citizenship(s).
fields.sourceRef: Application form, Personal Data section, field 'Kodakondsus'; Identity Documents Act §9(3)(6)

fields.name: educationLevel
fields.label: Education (highest graduated level)
fields.description: Optional field, marked with an asterisk on the form.
fields.sourceRef: Application form, Personal Data section, field 'Haridus'

fields.name: nationality
fields.label: Nationality
fields.description: Optional field, marked with an asterisk on the form. Distinct from `citizenship` on the form's own layout (ethnic/national identity rather than the state whose citizenship is held).
fields.sourceRef: Application form, Personal Data section, field 'Rahvus'

fields.name: nativeLanguage
fields.label: Native language
fields.description: Optional field, marked with an asterisk on the form.
fields.sourceRef: Application form, Personal Data section, field 'Emakeel'

fields.name: contactAddress
fields.label: Contact address
fields.description: Street/farm, house number, apartment number; village/borough/city; parish; county; country.
fields.sourceRef: Application form, Contacts section, field 'Kontaktaadress'

fields.name: zipCode
fields.label: Zip code
fields.description: Postal code of the contact address.
fields.sourceRef: Application form, Contacts section, field 'Sihtnumber'

fields.name: contactEmail
fields.label: E-mail
fields.description: The instructions page states this is provided 'if possible' — used to notify the applicant when the document is ready (unless it is instead ordered to a foreign representation).
fields.sourceRef: Application form, Contacts section, field 'e-post'; PPA 'Instructions for completing application form'

fields.name: phoneNumber
fields.label: Phone number
fields.description: Contact phone number at which the PPA can reach the applicant regarding the application, if necessary.
fields.sourceRef: Application form, Contacts section, field 'Telefon'

fields.name: passportRequestReason
fields.label: Reason for the passport request
fields.description: The reason the Estonian citizen's passport (travel document) is being requested, one of the form's own five checkbox options for the 'Travel document' row.
fields.sourceRef: Application form, 'Documents being applied for and places of issue' section, Travel document row, field 'pass põhjus'

fields.name: lastDocumentLossOrTheftDate
fields.label: Date of loss or theft of the last document
fields.description: Date the applicant's previous document was lost, destroyed, or stolen, per the instructions page's field-completion guidance for this scenario.
fields.sourceRef: PPA 'Instructions for completing application form' — 'Lost or stolen document data'

fields.name: citizenshipProofMethod
fields.label: Method of proving Estonian citizenship (first application only)
fields.description: When applying for a passport for the first time, an Estonian document proving citizenship must be submitted: either the applicant's own identity document, or an original document showing that a parent or grandparent holds Estonian citizenship (submitted together with a certificate proving the blood relationship, e.g. a birth certificate).
fields.sourceRef: PPA 'Estonian passport for an adult' — 'Applying for a passport for the first time'

fields.name: placeOfIssue
fields.label: Place of issue
fields.description: The PPA service office (or foreign representation, out of scope here) where the applicant wishes to claim the finished passport.
fields.sourceRef: Application form, 'Documents being applied for and places of issue' section, Travel document row, field 'Väljastuskoht 3'

fields.name: expeditedProcedureRequested
fields.label: Expedited (1 working day) procedure requested
fields.description: Whether the passport is requested under the expedited procedure (ready in a maximum of 1 working day, claimable only at the Tallinn service office) rather than the ordinary procedure (a maximum of 30 days). Not a checkbox on the AcroForm itself; the choice is made when booking/paying at the service office. See `documents.stateFeePayment` for the fee this affects.
fields.sourceRef: PPA 'Estonian passport for an adult' — 'Applying for passport under expedited procedure'

fields.name: feeDiscountEligibility
fields.label: State fee discount eligibility
fields.description: Whether the applicant qualifies for a reduced state fee: having reached Estonia's general statutory retirement age, or holding a moderate/severe/profound disability. Leave unset ('none') for the standard fee.
fields.sourceRef: PPA 'Estonian passport for an adult' — 'State fees'

fields.name: applyingForOneYearValidityPassport
fields.label: Applying for a passport with a 1-year validity term
fields.description: A distinct pathway under Identity Documents Act §21¹: available only to an Estonian citizen previously issued an Estonian citizen's passport or identity card who is staying in a foreign state with no Estonian foreign representation, or for whom addressing one is disproportionately burdensome. Issued without fingerprint images, valid up to 1 year, and available only for two consecutive applications.
fields.sourceRef: Application form, page 2, 'SHALL BE COMPLETED BY AN APPLICANT FOR AN ESTONIAN CITIZEN'S PASSPORT WITH VALIDITY TERM OF 1 YEAR'; Identity Documents Act §21¹

fields.name: oneYearValidityJustificationBasis
fields.label: Justification basis (1-year validity passport)
fields.description: The form instructs the applicant to 'state one confirmation and add the justification' — exactly one of these two bases.
fields.sourceRef: Application form, page 2, fields 'Kinnitus 1' / 'Kinnitus 2'

fields.name: oneYearValidityJustificationDetails
fields.label: Justification details (1-year validity passport)
fields.description: Free-text justification supporting the confirmation chosen in `oneYearValidityJustificationBasis`.
fields.sourceRef: Application form, page 2, field 'Tekst'

fields.name: hasLegalRepresentative
fields.label: Application submitted by a legal representative
fields.description: An application for a child under 15 years of age or a person with restricted active legal capacity must be submitted by their legal representative (parent or guardian). An applicant at least 15 years old may submit the application independently.
fields.sourceRef: Application form, page 2, 'LEGAL REPRESENTATIVE'; Identity Documents Act §10(3), §12¹(3)-(5)

fields.name: legalRepresentativeFullName
fields.label: Legal representative — given name and surname
fields.description: Full name of the legal representative (parent or guardian) submitting the application on the applicant's behalf.
fields.sourceRef: Application form, page 2, field 'Esindaja nimi'

fields.name: legalRepresentativePersonalIdCodeOrDob
fields.label: Legal representative — personal code or date of birth
fields.description: Estonian personal identification code of the legal representative, or, in its absence, their date of birth.
fields.sourceRef: Application form, page 2, field 'Isikukood või sünniaeg2'

fields.name: representingInstitutionName
fields.label: Name of the representing institution
fields.description: Only completed where the legal representative is an institution (e.g. a guardianship authority) rather than a natural-person parent/guardian.
fields.sourceRef: Application form, page 2, field 'Esindav asutus'

fields.name: representingInstitutionRegistryCode
fields.label: Registry code of the representing institution
fields.description: Only completed where the legal representative is an institution.
fields.sourceRef: Application form, page 2, field 'Registrikood2'

documents.label: Digital colour photograph
documents.sourceRef: Application form, page 1 footer: 'minimum resolution of at least 1300 x 1600 pixels, file size 1-5 MB, file format JPG'

documents.label: Document proving Estonian citizenship (first application only)
documents.sourceRef: PPA 'Estonian passport for an adult' — 'Applying for a passport for the first time'

documents.label: Certificate proving blood relationship (e.g. birth certificate)
documents.sourceRef: PPA 'Estonian passport for an adult' — 'Applying for a passport for the first time'

documents.label: State fee for the Estonian citizen's passport
documents.sourceRef: PPA 'Estonian passport for an adult' — 'State fees'; Identity Documents Act §4¹. Standard fee EUR 60; EUR 25 where `feeDiscountEligibility` is 'retirement-age' or 'disability'; EUR 250 where `expeditedProcedureRequested` is true. `amount` here states only the standard, non-discounted, non-expedited fee — see VERIFICATION.md.

documents.label: Applicant's data-accuracy declaration
documents.statement: I confirm that all the provided data is correct. I am aware that the submission of incorrect data is punishable.
documents.issuingAuthority: Self-attested by the applicant or their legal representative
documents.sourceRef: Application form, pages 1 and 2, signature declaration

steps.title: Passport request and place of issue
